scope: Add a progress surface for the materialized 100-agent discovery pilot directory

decision_context.prior_state: The repo had a materialized cluster directory, but there was no direct way to inspect fill progress without opening the per-cluster files manually.
decision_context.decision: Add a progress command that reads the directory, counts filled agents and raw candidates, and emits both JSON and markdown status reports.
decision_context.why_now: The next real work is collection, so the repo needed a truthful progress surface before any agents start filling the cluster packets.

files_expected.purpose: Adds the directory progress summary and markdown rendering command.

planned_steps: Add a progress command for the materialized directory
Generate the initial empty progress snapshot
Verify the command with the focused regression suite

commands: $env:PYTHONPATH='src'; python -m chip_labs.cli mirofish-discovery-program-progress --input-dir research/meta/mirofish_discovery_pilot_100_clusters_2026-03-24 --output research/meta/MIROFISH_DISCOVERY_PROGRAM_PILOT_100_PROGRESS_2026-03-24.json --markdown-output research/meta/MIROFISH_DISCOVERY_PROGRAM_PILOT_100_PROGRESS_2026-03-24.md --title "MiroFish Discovery Pilot 100 Progress"
$env:PYTHONPATH='src'; python -m pytest tests/test_mirofish_discovery.py tests/test_watchtower.py tests/test_mirofish_portfolio.py tests/test_trend_prediction.py -q
expected_checks: The progress snapshot reports 10 clusters and 100 total agents
The initial fill baseline remains 0 filled agents and 0 raw candidates
The focused regression suite stays green

guardrails: Do not fabricate discovered candidates while generating the progress baseline
Keep the progress read derived only from the materialized cluster directory
Preserve the empty baseline as the truthful starting state
